title: ononis repens subsp. antiquorum
intro: Ononis repens subsp. antiquorum, also known as the 'Burmedos' or 'creeping restharrow', is a subspecies of the Ononis repens plant, a member of the legume family, typically found in dry areas and coastal regions. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Ononis repens subsp. antiquorum include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Ononis repens subsp. antiquorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
